bead.title: Tighten gap-to-implementation handoff in align and check modes
bead.description:
Per PRD R-3, the alignment skill should produce a structured report AND a plan ordered by authority. Today the Align contract in skills/helix/SKILL.md says 'create or identify follow-up work for every non-aligned gap' but doesn't specify what the handoff to implementation looks like in runtime-neutral terms.

Update the Align and Check contracts so that for every non-aligned gap classified INCOMPLETE / UNDERSPECIFIED / DIVERGENT / STALE_PLAN the output names:
- the destination artifact type (frame, design, test, etc.) for resolving the gap
- the deliverable shape (e.g. 'a TD section answering X', 'a US covering Y')
- the suggested next workflow mode (frame, design, polish, build) — not a CLI command
- evidence references (paths + line numbers) supporting the finding

Avoid prescribing any runtime command. The plan output must be readable as a checklist a human can review in under 10 minutes (PRD success criterion 2).
bead.acceptance: skills/helix/SKILL.md §Align lists the four fields the gap-to-impl handoff must name (artifact type, deliverable shape, suggested next mode, evidence). §Check references the same shape when recommending next action. Both sections remain CLI-free. The contract is testable: running the skill against docs/helix/ produces a plan whose entries carry all four fields.
